item.title: Santos keeps CCS dream alive offshore Timor-Leste
item.description: Australian operator needs Bayu-Undan to absorb carbon dioxide vented at Barossa field

item.title: Sinopec secures new acreage in domestic exploration push
item.description: State-owned major secures three more onshore blocks this year as it expands its domestic acreage portfolio

item.title: Contractor line-up revealed for expansion project at world's largest gas field
item.description: The North Field, along with the extended South Pars field on the Iranian side of the Persian Gulf, is by far the world's largest non-associated natural gas field

item.title: Chinese player scoops PTTEP’s Myanmar platforms prize
item.description: Hilong will supply two platforms for Thai operator’s long-awaited Block M3 project

item.title: Largest oil play in PNG? Santos champing at the bit to drill
item.description: Mosa prospect offers fast-track development potential via existing operated infrastructure

item.title: Shell’s LNG Canada gives preliminary green light for phase two expansion
item.description: Fluor, JGC given limited notice to proceed for project to double facility’s capacity

item.title: BP’s head of gas and low carbon energy set to leave in latest leadership shakeup
item.description: William Lin’s departure by the end of the third quarter comes amid ongoing strategy reset and former chair Manifold’s exit

item.title: BP-controlled player to invest $680 million to revitalise Argentina’s largest oilfield
item.description: Pan American Energy seeks incentives for Cerro Dragon conventional field

item.title: Newfoundland study identifies 16 Tcf of additional recoverable gas
item.description: Latest study reinforces gas potential of proven Jeanne d'Arc basin, with royalty regime being developed

item.title: Oil surges after Iran pauses negotiations with US following Israel’s Lebanon incursion
item.description: Brent, WTI spike after reports of Iran’s plan to continue closing off Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Former Equinor executive bolsters Orsted's top team
item.description: Equinor veteran joins Orsted as head of operational excellence after 15 years at Norwegian developer

item.title: Is TotalEnergies walking away from offshore wind? France suggests otherwise
item.description: Oil and gas major seeks permit for 1.5GW wind farm off Normandy after giving up US sites and doubting conditions for multi-gigawatt German pipeline for wind at sea

item.title: US offshore wind faces fresh Trump threat from inspection fee plan
item.description: Proposal by Interior Department would add millions in costs for operating projects, far higher than equivalent oil and gas facilities

item.title: Weatherford to buy well optimisation player NCS
item.description: Acquisition of New York-listed NCS Multistage reportedly worth $151 million

item.title: NeoNext+ faces possible UK North Sea strike action
item.description: Workers will be balloted on strike action until 6 July

item.title: Dutch LNG terminal moves closer to import capacity expansion
item.description: The lifetime of Eemshaven LNG terminal extended to 2036 from 2027

item.title: Venezuela player's stock price lights up on London debut
item.description: Brothers Greig and Scott Gilbert plan to acquire oil and gas assets in hydrocarbon powerhouse

item.title: Four-way race in Petrobras tender ends with narrow $1000-margin victory
item.description: Bidding competition for flotel to operate offshore Brazil finishes with minimal gap

item.title: ExxonMobil seeking offshore rig for Canada exploration campaign
item.description: US supermajor plans to drill up to two exploration wells in Newfoundland & Labrador

item.title: BP starts gas production at Azerbaijan's largest oilfield, readies to take on new asset
item.description: UK supermajor aims to produce more natural gas to send to Europe

item.title: Kazakhstan orders second largest oilfield to revise turnaround plan and keep operating
item.description: Country's Prime Minister tells Kashagan field operator to postpone long-planned turnaround to 2027

item.title: Deepwater drillers agree to merge
item.description: Vantage and Eldorado have agreed to join forces under the name Vantage Drilling International

item.title: Deepwater rig en route back to Norway following BOP retrieval
item.description: Deepsea Atlantic needs repairs following 18 April incident

item.title: BW Energy turns on the boosters to quadruple production
item.description: Norwegian company excels in the Atlantic margin with its successful development model

item.title: Leading contenders emerge for multibillion-dollar deals on disputed Middle East gas project
item.description: Dorra offshore gas field being developed by joint venture comprising subsidiaries of Aramco and Kuwait Petroleum Corporation (KPC)

item.title: Schedule set for ConocoPhillips’ potential buy-out of Australian asset
item.description: Independent expert appointed to value offshore stake

item.title: Australian shale player completes merger with compatriot
item.description: Tamboran Resources expands its acreage and plans for at least four wells to be drilled this year

item.title: Criterium re-awards pipeline gig to keep gas development on track
item.description: Olindo comes on board at Southeast Mengoepeh in Indonesia, after prior deficiencies derail start-up to Q3

item.title: Seatrium in talks with SBM over potential future FPSOs work
item.description: Leading FPSO contractors mulling future workscopes despite Seatrium missing out on the SEAP 1 engineering, procurement and construction prize

item.title: SBM Offshore wins multibillion-dollar FPSO contracts for Petrobras project
item.description: Deals for the P-87 and P-81 floaters destined for the Sergipe-Alagoas basin are worth around $7.8 billion
